channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Inside Khayelitsha’s Mteto’s Child Villa: Contemporary Luxury in the Township
item.description: Khayelitsha, Cape Town’s largest township, now has a new upmarket accommodation option with the opening of Mteto’s Child Villa. Owned by local entrepreneur Luzuko Mteto, the villa offers five suites, ...

item.title: New Keytruda Injection Set to Reduce Hospital Time for Cancer Patients
item.description: A new injectable form of the immunotherapy drug Keytruda is set to significantly reduce the time NHS cancer patients spend in hospital. Traditionally administered via an intravenous drip taking over a...

item.title: Zulu nationalism and xenophobic attacks undermine pan-African unity
item.description: Ben Molapo discusses the dangers posed by narrow Zulu nationalism to the broader pan-African agenda. He argues that recent xenophobic attacks on African migrants in South Africa are a continuation of ...

item.title: David Benavidez dominates Zurdo Ramirez to become three-division world champion with a sixth-round knockout
item.description: David Benavidez put on a dominant performance against Gilberto 'Zurdo' Ramirez, securing a sixth-round knockout to claim the WBA and WBO cruiserweight titles. With this victory, Benavidez becomes a th...

item.title: Finance Ministry directs surrender of unutilised funds before budget finalisation
item.description: The Ministry of Finance has instructed all federal ministries, divisions, departments, and autonomous bodies to return any unspent allocations and anticipated savings by May 10. This move aims to fina...

item.title: Obi and Kwankwaso formally defect to NDC as opposition realignment builds for 2027
item.description: Former Anambra State governor, Peter Obi, and former Kano State governor, Rabiu Kwankwaso, have officially joined the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) in what many observers see as a major political ...

item.title: Historic Greek-run cafés and milk bars that shaped Australia’s dining culture
item.description: This photo essay highlights the lasting cultural and social impact of Greek-run cafés and milk bars across Australia, particularly from the 1930s onwards. Established by Greek immigrants in both metro...

item.title: Punjab police officers transferred to challenging areas in KP and Balochistan despite concerns
item.description: LAHORE: Punjab police officers are facing difficulties following a recent transfer order by the Establishment Division, which has sent 10 officers to what are termed 'hard areas' in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa...

item.title: Rudy Giuliani in Hospital with Serious Health Concerns
item.description: Rudy Giuliani, the former mayor of New York City and a prominent political figure, has been hospitalized and is currently in critical condition, according to a statement from his spokesperson. The new...

item.title: Tensions rise in NFP–MKP coalition in KwaZulu-Natal over Mbali Shinga dispute
item.description: The coalition between the National Freedom Party (NFP) and the MK Party (MKP) in KwaZulu-Natal is under increasing strain after the NFP upheld an appeal by provincial chair Mbali Shinga, halting her e...

item.title: UK to negotiate joining EU's €90bn loan programme to support Ukraine
item.description: The United Kingdom has announced plans to start negotiations to participate in a €90 billion (£78 billion) European Union loan scheme aimed at supporting Ukraine. Sir Keir Starmer is expected to outli...

item.title: South African Relay Teams Qualify for World Championships After Strong Performance in Botswana
item.description: South Africa's men's relay teams delivered impressive performances at the World Athletics Relays in Gaborone, Botswana, securing spots in next year's World Athletics Championships in Beijing. The men'...

item.title: Ongoing Tensions in the Middle East: May 4, 2026 Report
item.description: As of May 4, 2026, tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate, with the Strait of Hormuz, near Musandam, Oman, becoming a focal point for international attention. Recent reports indicate increas...

item.title: Tensions persist near Strait of Hormuz as Iran considers US response to 14‑point peace plan
item.description: In rolling coverage from The Sydney Morning Herald and The Age, Australia’s national news blog flags a fresh incident near the Strait of Hormuz where a cargo ship reported being attacked by multiple s...

item.title: House Justice Committee to Approve Report on Sara Duterte Impeachment Case
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – The House Committee on Justice is set to reconvene on May 4, 2026, to vote on a consolidated committee report regarding the impeachment case against Vice President Sara Duterte. ...

item.title: NAB CEO cautions on possible recession amid oil-driven inflation pressures
item.description: Andrew Irvine, CEO of National Australia Bank (NAB), has indicated that a recession in Australia is possible, although not probable, due to rising business costs and inflation linked to a global oil c...

item.title: AJK Government grants land rights to displaced families from LoC
item.description: In a significant move for internally displaced persons (IDPs) of Azad Jammu and Kashmir, Prime Minister Raja Faisal Mumtaz Rathore has formally granted ownership rights to over 1,000 families who had ...

item.title: Police warn of legal risks after social media ‘speedrun’ trend targets Vancouver Scientology site
item.description: A social media trend known as “speed running,” where participants attempt to race through Church of Scientology buildings while avoiding security, has reached Vancouver and prompted a police response....

item.title: Passenger detained after attacking flight attendant and attempting to enter cockpit on United Airlines flight
item.description: A 48-year-old man was detained after allegedly attacking a flight attendant and attempting to access the cockpit aboard United Airlines Flight 1837 as it landed at Newark Liberty International Airport...

item.title: Justin Clarke Redeems Himself with Knockout Victory Over PFL Heavyweight Abdoulaye Kane
item.description: Justin Clarke, South Africa’s rising MMA heavyweight in the Professional Fighters League (PFL) Africa, has bounced back spectacularly after his first professional loss. Clarke, 38, overcame Senegalese...
